author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice
book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice.
The Canons of the CCXVII Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage. Commonly Called The Code of Canons of the African Church.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice

title: Canon XXXVII.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It is not lawful to offer anything in the Holy Mysteries except bread and wine mixed with water.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: In the sacraments of the body and blood of the Lord nothing else shall be offered than that which the Lord himself ordained, that is to say, bread and wine mixed with water.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: But let the first-fruits, whether honey or milk, be offered on that one most solemn day, as is wont, in the mystery of the infants.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: For although they are offered on the altar, let them have nevertheless their own benediction, that they may be distinguished from the sacraments of the Lord’s body and blood; neither let there be offered as first-fruits anything other than grapes and corns.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Let bread and wine mixed with water only be offered.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The text of the Greek here does not exactly agree with the Latin.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: The Greek reads as follows: “That in the Holy Mysteries nothing else be offered than the body and blood of the Lord, even as the Lord himself delivered, that is bread and wine mixed with water.”

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Further down with regard to the first-fruits I have followed the Greek text which seems decidedly preferable, in fact the Latin is so corrupt that Van Espen notes that for the ordinary “offerantur” some mss. read “non offerantur.”

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: This canon is Canon xxiij. of the Synod of Hippo, a.d.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: “The Mystery of Infants” of this Quære, all that I have met with are in the dark as to this matter.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Dionysius Exiguus’s Latin is Lac, etc. The Greek stands thus, ῎Ειτε ·γάλα κ.τ.λ.
